title: 6H: browser smoke harness for isolated prototypes
goal: Add a standalone Playwright smoke harness for the Exercise Lab that discovers prototypes and exercises the complete pupil learning loop in system Chrome at desktop and an actual 320px touch viewport. Keep it generic and verification-only; do not create a shared exercise runtime or alter classroom code.
acceptance: The lab exposes an npm run test:smoke command using a locked Playwright dependency and system Chrome or a documented equivalent, without changing existing validate/check/build semantics.

The harness discovers prototype IDs and program-per-6h metadata from the catalog or a generated manifest rather than hardcoding IDs; SMOKE_EXERCISES may select exactly the three IDs owned by a paired review.

The harness defines a documented, presentation-neutral smoke contract made only of data attributes on visible enabled pupil controls and observable exercise states; every new 6H prototype uses it without importing a shared exercise runtime. Legacy EX-0001 receives a load/focus compatibility smoke only.

For each 6H prototype, a desktop scenario uses Tab/Enter/Space rather than click to complete the primary loop through an incorrect response, specific feedback, retry/recovery, correct response, continuation, and completion.

For each 6H prototype, a separate browser context with viewport exactly 320px wide and touch enabled taps through incorrect feedback, retry, correct response, continuation, and completion; it also fails on horizontal document overflow or hidden/off-screen required controls.

The entire exercised path fails on uncaught page/console errors, external or undeclared network requests, localStorage/sessionStorage writes, IndexedDB databases, cookies, or service-worker registration—not only during initial load.

The smoke contract and harness fail if required controls or feedback are hidden, disabled, duplicated ambiguously, keyboard-inoperable, or if success/error state is exposed only by color; reduced-motion emulation is covered by at least one complete scenario.
The existing EX-0001 compatibility smoke plus catalog validation, strict check, the complete filtered 6H path, and production build pass.
must_ask_if: The pinned decision, research, production-plan, or slice-graph inputs in experiments/design/6h-batch-inputs.json do not match the working baseline.
Installing the browser-test dependency or using system Chrome is unavailable, conflicts with repository package policy, or requires credentials/permissions.
A reliable smoke harness would require changing the Exercise Lab runtime, catalog schema, an existing exercise, classroom code, or files outside declared areas.
The proposed harness would send data externally or persist pupil/test state.
